destinations: External APIs (potentially, depending on shortlink bypass logic)

issues.description: Script uses GM_xmlhttpRequest to interact with external shortlink services, potentially transmitting user data, cookies, or page context to third-party domains.
issues.location: Main script logic (not fully visible, but inferred from purpose and grants)
issues.recommendation: Ensure only minimal, non-sensitive data is sent; avoid transmitting cookies or user-identifiable information.

issues.description: Script requests GM_openInTab, GM_setClipboard, GM_xmlhttpRequest, and window.onurlchange grants, which are high-privilege and can be abused if not strictly controlled.
issues.recommendation: Reduce granted permissions to only those strictly necessary for functionality.

issues.description: Script loads external code via @require from update.greasyfork.org, which is not version-hashed and could be replaced or tampered with.
issues.recommendation: Pin @require to a version hash or trusted CDN; verify integrity of external dependencies.

issues.description: Potential DOM XSS risk: script likely manipulates innerHTML/outerHTML to inject destination links or bypass mechanisms, which could be vulnerable if user input or URL parameters are not sanitized.
issues.location: Main script logic (not fully visible, but inferred from purpose)
issues.recommendation: Sanitize all user-controlled input before inserting into DOM.

details: Possible reading of cookies, localStorage, or sessionStorage to extract shortlink tokens or bypass mechanisms.

summary: The script 'Bypass All Shortlinks' requests broad permissions and interacts with third-party shortlink domains, potentially transmitting user data and exposing privacy risks. It also loads external code without version pinning, and its DOM manipulation logic may be vulnerable to XSS if not properly sanitized. The script is not recommended for use in sensitive environments without further review and hardening.
